rubric_items.reasoning: The agent applied the canonical nginx-ingress mechanism for rate limiting — the `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/limit-rps` annotation — directly on the `rate-api` Ingress, which the controller reconciles into a durable `limit_req_zone`/`limit_req` directive in nginx.conf. This is a root-cause, declarative fix (not an ephemeral pod-level hack) that will survive controller restarts and reconciliation. The agent confirmed this by reading the live nginx.conf and showing the exact zone/rate/burst config scoped only to the /api location, then confirmed 0 limit_req references for /health. Traffic tests independently confirmed 429/503 throttling on /api and unbroken 200s on /health. Minor deduction only because burst=2 nodelay allows short bursts above the nominal 2rps, but this is inherent to and expected from the requested annotation approach and still satisfies 'approximately 2 rps'.

The visible portion of the log shows a tight, logical sequence: diagnose (found neither ingress had rate-limit annotations, correctly identified and ignored the decoy `rate-api-alt-hard` for a different host), apply a scoped annotation fix, verify the generated nginx.conf directly rather than trusting the annotation alone, then run explicit before/after traffic tests on both /api and /health. No backtracking, no wrong turns, no wasted exploration — reached the goal in only 12 turns.

Used read-only `kubectl exec ... cat nginx.conf`/`grep` to inspect controller state, and the provided curl-test pod to validate live behavior — least-privilege, targeted commands throughout. The only mutation was a scoped annotation on the single relevant Ingress resource; no broad patches, deletes, or unnecessary cluster-wide reads.

Exactly one mutation for the entire task (per the K8s activity summary: 1 mutation out of 36 calls) — a two-line annotation patch to the single Ingress that needed it. The decoy ingress and the application Deployment were explicitly left untouched, and the agent called this out in its submission. This is about as minimal and focused as the fix could be.
